pi_items.source: Task specification and FreeCAD environment convention
Mathematical calculation: helix centerline length (454.15 mm) × circular cross-section area (π × 1² mm²)
Standard engineering tolerance for spring manufacturing
Standard engineering tolerance for dimensional accuracy in FreeCAD models
Task description: 'Wire cross-section: circular with radius 1 mm'
Task description: 'Helix radius: 9 mm (measured to the center of the wire)'
Task description: 'Number of turns: 8 (total helix height = 40 mm)'
Task description: 'Helix pitch: 5 mm' and verified by 40 mm height / 8 turns = 5 mm/turn

privileged_info_summary: The compression spring task requires creating a helical spring in FreeCAD with precise specifications: helix radius 9 mm (to wire center), wire radius 1 mm, 8 turns, and 5 mm pitch. The resulting geometry has a bounding box of 20 mm × 20 mm × 42 mm and a volume of approximately 1427 mm³. All metadata values have been independently verified through geometric calculations. The helix centerline length is 454.15 mm, and the cross-sectional area of the circular wire is π mm². The task is synthetic with no external data dependencies; all values are derived from the specified parameters and are mathematically consistent.
